name: Kanye Omari West
early_life: Born in Atlanta, Georgia, parents divorced when he was three, moved to Chicago with his mother. Father was a former Black Panther and photojournalist. Mother was an English professor. Raised in a middle-class environment. Lived in Nanjing, China for a year at age 10, where his mother taught. Showed early artistic talent: poetry at five, rapping at third grade, composing music by seventh grade. Mentored by producer No I.D. Briefly attended American Academy of Art and Chicago State University before dropping out to pursue music.
defining_wounds: Jaw shattered in a 2002 car accident, inspiring 'Through the Wire'.
core_personality: Deeply sensitive, particularly regarding his mother
Strong Christian faith, especially later in life
musical_style: Melds multiple genres (electropop, acid-house, drill, industrial, gospel, Christian rap, psychedelic)
Lyrical themes: higher education, materialism, self-consciousness, class struggle, family, race, faith, excess
Rhymes described as funny, provocative, articulate, with seamless shifts from commentary to braggadocio to sensitivity
other_ventures: Fashion design: Yeezy (Nike, Louis Vuitton, Adidas, Gap Inc.), collaborations with Giuseppe Zanotti, M/M Paris.
Co-owner of music streaming service Tidal.
Attempted acquisition of social network Parler.
Owned Fatburger restaurants in Chicago area.
Aspired to open an architecture firm.
philanthropy: Kanye West Foundation (later The Dr. Donda West Foundation) focused on battling dropout and illiteracy rates, providing music education.
Donda's House, Inc. to help at-risk Chicago youth.
Donated $10 million to artist James Turrell's Roden Crater project.
Donated $2 million to George Floyd's family and other victims of police brutality (Ahmaud Arbery, Breonna Taylor) in 2020.
acting_and_filmmaking: Cameo appearances in 'State Property 2', 'The Love Guru', 'Entourage', 'Anchorman 2'.
Voice role as 'Kenny West' in 'The Cleveland Show'.
Starred in short films: 'We Were Once a Fairytale' (2009), 'Runaway' (2010).
relationship_with_press: Antagonistic and volatile. Frequently clashes with media outlets due to controversial statements and actions. Has boycotted interviews and at times engaged in hostile exchanges.
public_persona: Known for public outbursts and controversial commentary on music, fashion, politics, race, and slavery
Often seen as arrogant and self-aggrandizing, but also as a misunderstood genius
how_they_saw_themself: A visionary artist and innovator, ahead of his time.
A god-like figure, destined for greatness.
A misunderstood genius, battling against the establishment.
A voice for the voiceless, a cultural icon.
An important figure in religion and spirituality.
contradictions: Expresses deep Christian faith while engaging in controversial and at times blasphemous behavior.
Criticizes materialism while amassing significant wealth and promoting luxury brands.
Advocates for authenticity while often engaging in calculated public performances.
Fights against the music industry establishment while seeking massive commercial success.
Demonstrates a profound appreciation for Black culture and history, but has also made antisemitic remarks and sympathized with Nazi ideology.

relationships.mother: Donda West, his late mother, was a profound influence and muse; her death deeply affected him.

notable_awards_and_recognitions: Named one of Time's 100 most influential people in 2005 and 2015.
Six albums listed in Rolling Stone's '500 Greatest Albums of All Time'.
Named one of Rolling Stone's '100 Greatest Songwriters of All Time'.
controversies: George W. Bush 'he doesn't care about black people' comment.
MTV VMA stage crash for 'Touch the Sky' (2006).
Antisemitic remarks and sympathy for Nazi ideology (2022–2024), leading to loss of sponsorships.
Unsuccessful presidential campaign and associated rhetoric.
Public feuds with numerous celebrities and industry figures.
Behavior leading to hospitalization for temporary psychosis (2016).
speech_patterns: Mix of profound pronouncements and colloquialisms.
Occasional use of religious or philosophical language.
Can be both charmingly witty and aggressively confrontational.
